33	## Origins
34	
35	### Early Life
36	
37	Petyr's [great-grandfather](wiki:Baelish_(sellsword)) was a [Braavosi](wiki:Braavosi) [sellsword](wiki:Sellsword) in the service of Lord [Corbray](wiki:House_Corbray); when his [son](wiki:Baelish_(hedge_knight)) became a [hedge knight](wiki:Hedge_knight), he took the stone head of the [Titan of Braavos](wiki:Titan_of_Braavos) as his sigil. Petyr's [father](wiki:Baelish_(lord)), in turn, was the smallest of small lords of a few rocky acres on the smallest of the [Fingers](wiki:Fingers),(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-7) Petyr's mother was named [Alayne](wiki:Alayne_Baelish).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-5) Petyr's father once took him to a hermit's cave to see a man said to have the gift of [prophecy](wiki:Prophecy). Lord Baelish gave the hermit a skin of [wine](wiki:Wine) after he groped the boy a bit and said he would be a great man.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-6)
38	
39	Petyr's father befriended [Hoster Tully](wiki:Hoster_Tully) during his service in the [War of the Ninepenny Kings](wiki:War_of_the_Ninepenny_Kings), and this friendship led to Petyr being sent to be fostered as a [ward](wiki:Ward) at [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-2)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rssm1381.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_31-0)
40	
41	### Riverrun
42	
43	Petyr was raised alongside Lord [Hoster Tully](wiki:Hoster_Tully)'s children, [Catelyn](wiki:Catelyn_Stark), [Lysa](wiki:Lysa_Arryn), and [Edmure](wiki:Edmure_Tully). Because of Petyr's family holdings and his short stature, Edmure gave him the nickname "Littlefinger," by which he is still called.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-9) Petyr grew close to the Tully children,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-8) and he played with Edmure(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-1) and his sisters. Catelyn recalls how she and Lysa once made mud pies, which Petyr ate, resulting in him being sick for a week.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot28.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_32-0) When he had a problem, Petyr went to Lord Hoster's brother, Ser [Brynden Tully](wiki:Brynden_Tully), just as Lord Hoster's children were wont to do.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot34.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_33-0) Petyr and Edmure once built a castle out of blocks in Hoster's solar.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-2)
44	
45	When Catelyn was twelve, Petyr accompanied the Tullys on a journey to [Seagard](wiki:Seagard). They camped at [Oldstones](wiki:Oldstones), where Catelyn pretended to be [Jenny of Oldstones](wiki:Jenny_of_Oldstones) and Petyr her [Prince of Dragonflies](wiki:Prince_of_Dragonflies).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_34-0) Catelyn and Lysa became separated from the rest of their party while riding back from Seagard, but Petyr rode back and found the girls in the fog.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_35-0)
46	
47	As the children grew older, Petyr fell in love with Catelyn,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-4) who was several years his elder. Catelyn did not reciprocate Petyr's feelings, loving him as a brother instead.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-10)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_35-1) Catelyn's younger sister, Lysa, on the other hand, fell in love with Petyr.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-0) Petyr would play at kissing with both Catelyn and Lysa in the [godswood of Riverrun](wiki:Godswood_of_Riverrun), attempting to stick his tongue into their mouths. Catelyn stopped Petyr, but Lysa both allowed and enjoyed it.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-2)
48	
49	When Lords [Bracken](wiki:House_Bracken) and [Blackwood](wiki:House_Blackwood) were at Riverrun to settle a feud with Lord Tully's help, Catelyn and Petyr danced six dances. However, when he tried to kiss her, she pushed him away and laughed at him. Petyr, wounded by the rejection, got drunk, passed out at the table, and was brought to his bed by Brynden Tully. Lysa snuck in after him, and gave Petyr her [maidenhead](wiki:Maiden) that night. Still drunk, Petyr believed he was having sex with Catelyn, and he called Lysa "Cat" and told her he loved her before falling asleep.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-1)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-39)
50	
51	In [282 AC](wiki:282_AC), when the date of Catelyn's [wedding](wiki:Wedding) was announced,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-4) Petyr, scarcely fifteen years old, challenged the twenty-year-old Brandon Stark to a duel for her hand.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-11) Petyr arrived with only a helm, breastplate and mail, leading to Brandon taking off most of his own armor. Petyr asked Catelyn for her favor to wear, but she refused, giving it to Brandon instead.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-2)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_40-0) Catelyn begged Brandon not to kill Petyr, to which he agreed. While Brandon offered Petyr multiple chances to yield, Petyr refused to give up. In the end, Petyr was staggering and bleeding from a dozen wounds, when Brandon ended the battle with a backhand cut so brutal Catelyn was convinced it was mortal.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_40-1)
52	
53	Petyr was given a fortnight to recover at Riverrun, during which time Lysa helped care for him. Catelyn did not go to see him, and Petyr refused to see Edmure, who had served as Brandon's squire during the duel.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_40-2)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_41-0) During this fortnight, Petyr slept with Lysa again.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-5) He would later claim to have taken the maidenhood of both Tully sisters, due to his belief that his first encounter with Lysa had actually been with Catelyn.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot31.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_42-0)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-9)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_35-2)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rssm1172.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_43-0)
54	
55	When the fortnight had passed and Petyr was strong enough, Lord Hoster sent Petyr away from Riverrun. Catelyn did not say goodbye to him.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_40-3) However, Lysa had become pregnant with Petyr's child as a result of their second sexual encounter. After Petyr had been sent away, Lysa revealed her pregnancy to her father, hoping he would relent and allow Lysa and Petyr to marry. Instead, Hoster, who had believed Petyr to be too lowborn,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-7) forced Lysa to drink [moon tea](wiki:Moon_tea) to abort the pregnancy.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos2.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_44-0)
56	
57	Back at the [Fingers](wiki:Fingers), Petyr sent Catelyn a single letter after the death of her betrothed, Brandon. However, she burned the letter without reading it.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-12) Catelyn and Lysa married Lords [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark) and [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn), respectively, in a [double wedding at Riverrun](wiki:Weddings_of_Catelyn_and_Lysa_Tully) during [Robert's Rebellion](wiki:Robert's_Rebellion),(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot2.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_45-0) and Catelyn went north to [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell) after the conclusion of the war.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_37-1) Petyr used to dream about Winterfell, always imagining it as a dark and cold place.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-3)
58	
59	### Gulltown and King's Landing
60	
61	[Lysa Arryn](wiki:Lysa_Arryn) convinced [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn), her new husband and [Lord of the Eyrie](wiki:Lord_of_the_Eyrie), to give Petyr the customs at [Gulltown](wiki:Gulltown)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-8) as his first post(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-4) to please her,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-9) and the young man had control of the city's port.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-10) According to Lysa, Petyr increased incomes tenfold.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-11) Lysa convinced Jon to bring Petyr to court in [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-5) According to [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), in [289 AC](wiki:289_AC) Jon gave Petyr a minor sinecure in customs, where Petyr brought in three times as much as other royal collectors.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-10) Lord Arryn, also the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King) for [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon), rewarded Petyr with other appointments.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-12) Within three years of coming to the capital, Littlefinger had become [master of coin](wiki:Master_of_coin) and a member of the [small council](wiki:Small_council).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-11)
62	
63	Petyr increased his influence by moving his own people in place, such as the four [Keepers of the Keys](wiki:Keeper_of_the_Keys), the [King's Counter](wiki:King's_Counter), and the [King's Scales](wiki:King's_Scales), as well as harbor masters, toll collectors, and [wine](wiki:Wine) factors.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-12) Meanwhile, he also developed a complex web of loans, transactions, and investments, as to supply the king and the Hand with enough [gold](wiki:Currency),(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-13) and he bought a number of establishments (including several [brothels](wiki:Prostitution)).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_46-0)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok15.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-2) The crown's revenues have increased tenfold compared to Littlefinger's predecessor as master of coin, although the royal debts of King Robert are vast as well.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-14) The last [Chief Gaoler](wiki:Chief_Gaoler), a former cloth merchant, purchased his position from Petyr.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_47-0)
64	
65	Petyr bet on Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) during the [tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day](wiki:Tourney_on_Prince_Joffrey's_name_day), but he lost [his blade](wiki:Littlefinger's_blade) of [Valyrian steel](wiki:Valyrian_steel) to King Robert when Jaime was unhorsed by Ser [Loras Tyrell](wiki:Loras_Tyrell).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_35-3)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-6) Littlefinger has also boasted at court of having taken [Catelyn Stark](wiki:Catelyn_Stark)'s virginity.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot31.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_42-1) [Oswell Kettleblack](wiki:Oswell_Kettleblack) has been in Petyr's service for a long time.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc10.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_25-1)
66	
67	Living in the capital with the elderly Lord Arryn for years with Petyr nearby has been difficult for Lysa.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-13)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-7) She and Petyr started planning a life together,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-6) and according to a semi-canon source they have begun a secret affair.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-8)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiaflysa_arryn.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_48-0) When Lysa learned that her husband planned to send their son, [Robert Arryn](wiki:Robert_Arryn), away to be fostered at [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone) by Lord [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), Petyr provided her with the [poison](wiki:Tears_of_Lys) she used to kill Lord Jon. He also convinced Lysa to send a message to her sister Catelyn at Winterfell, in which she falsely accused Queen [Cersei](wiki:Cersei_Lannister) and [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) of the murder of her husband.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_37-2)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-7)
68	
69	## Appearances & Description
70	
71	Petyr had been slight and short for his age as a boy, and nearing his thirties, he is still a short man of slender build. Petyr has sharp features and a small pointed beard on his chin.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-3) His dark hair has threads of silver(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-4) running through it which are also described as a silver streak.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-1) He has laughing gray-green eyes(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-5)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok15.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) like a [cat](wiki:Cat),(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0) but [Sansa Stark](wiki:Sansa_Stark) notes that when Petyr laughs, his eyes do not.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-2) His breath is always fresh,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-2) smelling like mint,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-3) which he had enjoyed chewing as a boy.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0)
72	
73	Petyr has always been cunning and clever. As a child, he was bold, always getting into trouble,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-1) but was also capable of looking contrite after his mischiefs.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-6) While Petyr was born to the small and insignificant [House Baelish](wiki:House_Baelish), he is a subtle and ambitious man(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-1) whose intelligence helped him advance in terms of power and status.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0) He possesses considerable skill in commerce and [coin](wiki:Coin),(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-2)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-3) as well as manipulation, and has a gift for political improvisation.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-7)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0)
74	
75	Petyr is often underestimated by many high lords due to his low birth.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-3)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-1) According to [George R. R. Martin](wiki:George_R._R._Martin), "Everybody trusts him because he seems powerless, and he's very friendly, and he's very helpful."(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-17) Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) looks down upon Petyr(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0) and sees him as ambitious,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot8.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2) but does not consider him a threat.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0) While Lord [Orton Merryweather](wiki:Orton_Merryweather) finds Petyr to be witty and amusing,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0) he is considered dangerous by [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok15.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-1) and devious by Lord [Varys](wiki:Varys).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot58.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_21-0)
76	
77	Petyr owns a slashed velvet doublet in cream-and-[silver](wiki:Silver),(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot33.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-0) a plum-colored doublet with a [mockingbird](wiki:Mockingbird) embroidered on the breast in black thread,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-0) a blue velvet tunic with puffed sleeves,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_24-0) and a black velvet doublet with grey sleeves.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc10.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_25-0) He is partial to shades of rose and plum.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-4)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok65.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_26-0) His cloaks, which are clasped with a silver mockingbird,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-8) include grey [silk](wiki:Silk) trimmed with black [fox](wiki:Fox),(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot33.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-1) stripes of black and white,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-1) and a pattern of mockingbirds.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok65.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_26-1) Petyr also owns a yellow satin cape(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-5) and a silver cape(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot65.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_27-0) patterned with mockingbirds.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_24-1)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_28-0) He has a pair of red-dyed leather boots ornamented with black scrollwork.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-6) Petyr rides a mare(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-1) and once owned a [Valyrian steel dagger](wiki:Littlefinger's_blade).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-1)
78	
79	Petyr's chambers in the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) are located across the Great Yard from the [Tower of the Hand](wiki:Tower_of_the_Hand).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_29-0) He enjoys pomegranates(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-4) and drinks [wine](wiki:Wine).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-1)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc41.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_30-0)
80	
81	## Narrative Arc
82	
83	### A Game of Thrones
84	
85	Lady [Catelyn Stark](wiki:Catelyn_Stark) travels to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) to inform her husband, Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark), about the attempt on the life of their son [Bran](wiki:Bran_Stark). Petyr, having learned of her arrival despite her efforts to remain unnoticed, intercepts Catelyn and has her brought to the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep). Catelyn is wary of Petyr and Lord [Varys](wiki:Varys) when she shows them the [Valyrian steel](wiki:Valyrian_steel) dagger used in the attempt by a [catspaw](wiki:Catspaw). Petyr says that he recognizes the dagger, claiming that it was once his but that he lost it to [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister) in a wager during the [tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day](wiki:Tourney_on_Prince_Joffrey's_name_day).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-13) In truth, however, [Littlefinger's blade](wiki:Littlefinger's_blade) had been lost to King [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_35-4)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-9) Petyr promises Catelyn he will help her husband investigate the death of Lord [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn), Lord Stark's predecessor as [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_46-1)
86	
87	After a meeting of the [small council](wiki:Small_council), Petyr brings Eddard to meet with his wife, whom Littlefinger has hidden at one of his [brothels](wiki:Prostitution), and Petyr convinces the new Hand, against his inclination, to make no move against the [Lannisters](wiki:House_Lannister) until they have proof. Catelyn feels she has reconnected with her childhood friend.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_46-2) Eddard remains wary of Petyr after Catelyn departs the capital, but he becomes more trusting after Littlefinger advises him to investigate Lord Arryn's household. Petyr, however, warns Ned not to trust anyone, including himself.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot25.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_49-0)
88	
89	During the [Hand's tourney](wiki:Hand's_tourney), Petyr loses a hundred [golden dragons](wiki:Currency) to Lord [Renly Baratheon](wiki:Renly_Baratheon) when [Sandor Clegane](wiki:Sandor_Clegane) unhorses Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_29-1) Petyr meets [Sansa Stark](wiki:Sansa_Stark), the eldest daughter of Eddard and Catelyn, during the festivities, and he brushes the girl's cheek while touching her hair.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-5)
90	
91	Acting on Petyr's lie about the dagger, Catelyn arrests Tyrion at the [inn at the crossroads](wiki:Inn_at_the_crossroads)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot28.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_32-1) and takes him to the [Eyrie](wiki:Eyrie).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot34.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_33-1) Tyrion insists the dagger used by the assassin was not his, and he tells Catelyn that Petyr has often claimed at court to have taken her virginity.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot31.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_42-2)
92	
93	When King Robert learns that the exiled Princess [Daenerys Targaryen](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen) is pregnant, Petyr agrees with Robert in advocating her assassination. Ned resigns as Hand in protest of Robert's command, but before he can arrange to travel back to Winterfell, Petyr approaches him with the promise of assistance in his investigation into Jon Arryn's death.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot33.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-2) Littlefinger leads Ned to [a brothel](wiki:Chataya's_brothel) where [a young whore](wiki:Barra's_mother) is raising [one of Robert's bastard children](wiki:Barra). When Ned is accosted by [red cloaks](wiki:House_Lannister_guards) led by Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) while returning from the city shortly thereafter,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-2) Petyr flees to find [gold cloaks](wiki:City_Watch_of_King's_Landing), but Lord Stark is wounded and the [Hand's guards](wiki:House_Stark_guards) are killed in the meantime.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_50-0)
94	
95	Petyr attends court while Eddard sits the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) in the Robert's absence, as the king has gone hunting in the [kingswood](wiki:Kingswood).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_51-0) Petyr's flight from Jaime's men bothers Lord Stark.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-2)
96	
97	Robert is mortally wounded hunting a boar, and the dying king names Eddard as [Lord Regent](wiki:Regent) and [Protector of the Realm](wiki:Protector_of_the_Realm). Petyr advises Ned that despite their knowledge that Queen [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister)'s children are illegitimate and that Robert's brother, Lord [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), is the legal heir, the wisest course of action is to ensure Prince [Joffrey Baratheon](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon)'s succession, a maneuver calculated to avoid all-out war and maximize their own power. Eddard refuses the suggestion and instead enlists Petyr's help in securing the allegiance of the gold cloaks for when he moves against Cersei.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_24-2) Littlefinger actually bribes the gold cloaks to support the queen in the [throne room](wiki:Throne_room), however. As he recovers his dagger during the [arrest of Eddard Stark](wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark), Petyr reminds Ned he had told him not to trust him.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_28-1)
98	
99	Petyr wears black as the small council mourns Robert.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos51.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_52-0) As the majority of Eddard's followers in King's Landing have been killed following his arrest,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot50.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_53-0) Petyr takes [Vayon Poole](wiki:Vayon_Poole)'s daughter [Jeyne](wiki:Jeyne_Poole), Sansa's friend, into his custody.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot51.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_54-0) Littlefinger mocks Ser [Barristan Selmy](wiki:Barristan_Selmy) when the famed [knight](wiki:Knight) is publicly dismissed from the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot57.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_55-0)
100

## Identity

## Edges

## Origins

### Early Life

Petyr's [great-grandfather](wiki:Baelish_(sellsword)) was a [Braavosi](wiki:Braavosi) [sellsword](wiki:Sellsword) in the service of Lord [Corbray](wiki:House_Corbray); when his [son](wiki:Baelish_(hedge_knight)) became a [hedge knight](wiki:Hedge_knight), he took the stone head of the [Titan of Braavos](wiki:Titan_of_Braavos) as his sigil. Petyr's [father](wiki:Baelish_(lord)), in turn, was the smallest of small lords of a few rocky acres on the smallest of the [Fingers](wiki:Fingers),(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-7) Petyr's mother was named [Alayne](wiki:Alayne_Baelish).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-5) Petyr's father once took him to a hermit's cave to see a man said to have the gift of [prophecy](wiki:Prophecy). Lord Baelish gave the hermit a skin of [wine](wiki:Wine) after he groped the boy a bit and said he would be a great man.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-6)

Petyr's father befriended [Hoster Tully](wiki:Hoster_Tully) during his service in the [War of the Ninepenny Kings](wiki:War_of_the_Ninepenny_Kings), and this friendship led to Petyr being sent to be fostered as a [ward](wiki:Ward) at [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-2)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rssm1381.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_31-0)

### Riverrun

Petyr was raised alongside Lord [Hoster Tully](wiki:Hoster_Tully)'s children, [Catelyn](wiki:Catelyn_Stark), [Lysa](wiki:Lysa_Arryn), and [Edmure](wiki:Edmure_Tully). Because of Petyr's family holdings and his short stature, Edmure gave him the nickname "Littlefinger," by which he is still called.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-9) Petyr grew close to the Tully children,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-8) and he played with Edmure(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-1) and his sisters. Catelyn recalls how she and Lysa once made mud pies, which Petyr ate, resulting in him being sick for a week.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot28.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_32-0) When he had a problem, Petyr went to Lord Hoster's brother, Ser [Brynden Tully](wiki:Brynden_Tully), just as Lord Hoster's children were wont to do.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot34.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_33-0) Petyr and Edmure once built a castle out of blocks in Hoster's solar.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-2)

When Catelyn was twelve, Petyr accompanied the Tullys on a journey to [Seagard](wiki:Seagard). They camped at [Oldstones](wiki:Oldstones), where Catelyn pretended to be [Jenny of Oldstones](wiki:Jenny_of_Oldstones) and Petyr her [Prince of Dragonflies](wiki:Prince_of_Dragonflies).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_34-0) Catelyn and Lysa became separated from the rest of their party while riding back from Seagard, but Petyr rode back and found the girls in the fog.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_35-0)

As the children grew older, Petyr fell in love with Catelyn,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-4) who was several years his elder. Catelyn did not reciprocate Petyr's feelings, loving him as a brother instead.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-10)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_35-1) Catelyn's younger sister, Lysa, on the other hand, fell in love with Petyr.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-0) Petyr would play at kissing with both Catelyn and Lysa in the [godswood of Riverrun](wiki:Godswood_of_Riverrun), attempting to stick his tongue into their mouths. Catelyn stopped Petyr, but Lysa both allowed and enjoyed it.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-2)

When Lords [Bracken](wiki:House_Bracken) and [Blackwood](wiki:House_Blackwood) were at Riverrun to settle a feud with Lord Tully's help, Catelyn and Petyr danced six dances. However, when he tried to kiss her, she pushed him away and laughed at him. Petyr, wounded by the rejection, got drunk, passed out at the table, and was brought to his bed by Brynden Tully. Lysa snuck in after him, and gave Petyr her [maidenhead](wiki:Maiden) that night. Still drunk, Petyr believed he was having sex with Catelyn, and he called Lysa "Cat" and told her he loved her before falling asleep.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-1)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-39)

In [282 AC](wiki:282_AC), when the date of Catelyn's [wedding](wiki:Wedding) was announced,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-4) Petyr, scarcely fifteen years old, challenged the twenty-year-old Brandon Stark to a duel for her hand.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-11) Petyr arrived with only a helm, breastplate and mail, leading to Brandon taking off most of his own armor. Petyr asked Catelyn for her favor to wear, but she refused, giving it to Brandon instead.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-2)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_40-0) Catelyn begged Brandon not to kill Petyr, to which he agreed. While Brandon offered Petyr multiple chances to yield, Petyr refused to give up. In the end, Petyr was staggering and bleeding from a dozen wounds, when Brandon ended the battle with a backhand cut so brutal Catelyn was convinced it was mortal.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_40-1)

Petyr was given a fortnight to recover at Riverrun, during which time Lysa helped care for him. Catelyn did not go to see him, and Petyr refused to see Edmure, who had served as Brandon's squire during the duel.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_40-2)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_41-0) During this fortnight, Petyr slept with Lysa again.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-5) He would later claim to have taken the maidenhood of both Tully sisters, due to his belief that his first encounter with Lysa had actually been with Catelyn.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot31.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_42-0)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-9)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_35-2)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rssm1172.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_43-0)

When the fortnight had passed and Petyr was strong enough, Lord Hoster sent Petyr away from Riverrun. Catelyn did not say goodbye to him.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot40.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_40-3) However, Lysa had become pregnant with Petyr's child as a result of their second sexual encounter. After Petyr had been sent away, Lysa revealed her pregnancy to her father, hoping he would relent and allow Lysa and Petyr to marry. Instead, Hoster, who had believed Petyr to be too lowborn,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-7) forced Lysa to drink [moon tea](wiki:Moon_tea) to abort the pregnancy.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos2.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_44-0)

Back at the [Fingers](wiki:Fingers), Petyr sent Catelyn a single letter after the death of her betrothed, Brandon. However, she burned the letter without reading it.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-12) Catelyn and Lysa married Lords [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark) and [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn), respectively, in a [double wedding at Riverrun](wiki:Weddings_of_Catelyn_and_Lysa_Tully) during [Robert's Rebellion](wiki:Robert's_Rebellion),(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot2.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_45-0) and Catelyn went north to [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell) after the conclusion of the war.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_37-1) Petyr used to dream about Winterfell, always imagining it as a dark and cold place.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-3)

### Gulltown and King's Landing

[Lysa Arryn](wiki:Lysa_Arryn) convinced [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn), her new husband and [Lord of the Eyrie](wiki:Lord_of_the_Eyrie), to give Petyr the customs at [Gulltown](wiki:Gulltown)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-8) as his first post(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-4) to please her,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-9) and the young man had control of the city's port.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-10) According to Lysa, Petyr increased incomes tenfold.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-11) Lysa convinced Jon to bring Petyr to court in [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-5) According to [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister), in [289 AC](wiki:289_AC) Jon gave Petyr a minor sinecure in customs, where Petyr brought in three times as much as other royal collectors.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-10) Lord Arryn, also the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King) for [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon), rewarded Petyr with other appointments.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-12) Within three years of coming to the capital, Littlefinger had become [master of coin](wiki:Master_of_coin) and a member of the [small council](wiki:Small_council).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-11)

Petyr increased his influence by moving his own people in place, such as the four [Keepers of the Keys](wiki:Keeper_of_the_Keys), the [King's Counter](wiki:King's_Counter), and the [King's Scales](wiki:King's_Scales), as well as harbor masters, toll collectors, and [wine](wiki:Wine) factors.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-12) Meanwhile, he also developed a complex web of loans, transactions, and investments, as to supply the king and the Hand with enough [gold](wiki:Currency),(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-13) and he bought a number of establishments (including several [brothels](wiki:Prostitution)).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_46-0)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok15.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-2) The crown's revenues have increased tenfold compared to Littlefinger's predecessor as master of coin, although the royal debts of King Robert are vast as well.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-14) The last [Chief Gaoler](wiki:Chief_Gaoler), a former cloth merchant, purchased his position from Petyr.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc27.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_47-0)

Petyr bet on Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) during the [tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day](wiki:Tourney_on_Prince_Joffrey's_name_day), but he lost [his blade](wiki:Littlefinger's_blade) of [Valyrian steel](wiki:Valyrian_steel) to King Robert when Jaime was unhorsed by Ser [Loras Tyrell](wiki:Loras_Tyrell).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_35-3)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-6) Littlefinger has also boasted at court of having taken [Catelyn Stark](wiki:Catelyn_Stark)'s virginity.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot31.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_42-1) [Oswell Kettleblack](wiki:Oswell_Kettleblack) has been in Petyr's service for a long time.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc10.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_25-1)

Living in the capital with the elderly Lord Arryn for years with Petyr nearby has been difficult for Lysa.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-13)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-7) She and Petyr started planning a life together,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-6) and according to a semi-canon source they have begun a secret affair.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-8)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiaflysa_arryn.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_48-0) When Lysa learned that her husband planned to send their son, [Robert Arryn](wiki:Robert_Arryn), away to be fostered at [Dragonstone](wiki:Dragonstone) by Lord [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), Petyr provided her with the [poison](wiki:Tears_of_Lys) she used to kill Lord Jon. He also convinced Lysa to send a message to her sister Catelyn at Winterfell, in which she falsely accused Queen [Cersei](wiki:Cersei_Lannister) and [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) of the murder of her husband.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot6.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_37-2)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos80.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_36-7)

## Appearances & Description

Petyr had been slight and short for his age as a boy, and nearing his thirties, he is still a short man of slender build. Petyr has sharp features and a small pointed beard on his chin.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-3) His dark hair has threads of silver(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-4) running through it which are also described as a silver streak.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-1) He has laughing gray-green eyes(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-5)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok15.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0) like a [cat](wiki:Cat),(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0) but [Sansa Stark](wiki:Sansa_Stark) notes that when Petyr laughs, his eyes do not.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-2) His breath is always fresh,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-2) smelling like mint,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-3) which he had enjoyed chewing as a boy.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0)

Petyr has always been cunning and clever. As a child, he was bold, always getting into trouble,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-1) but was also capable of looking contrite after his mischiefs.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-6) While Petyr was born to the small and insignificant [House Baelish](wiki:House_Baelish), he is a subtle and ambitious man(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-1) whose intelligence helped him advance in terms of power and status.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-0) He possesses considerable skill in commerce and [coin](wiki:Coin),(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-2)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_15-0)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-3) as well as manipulation, and has a gift for political improvisation.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-7)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-0)

Petyr is often underestimated by many high lords due to his low birth.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-3)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-1) According to [George R. R. Martin](wiki:George_R._R._Martin), "Everybody trusts him because he seems powerless, and he's very friendly, and he's very helpful."(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-17) Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) looks down upon Petyr(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-0) and sees him as ambitious,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot8.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2) but does not consider him a threat.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc44.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_19-0) While Lord [Orton Merryweather](wiki:Orton_Merryweather) finds Petyr to be witty and amusing,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc17.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_20-0) he is considered dangerous by [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok15.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-1) and devious by Lord [Varys](wiki:Varys).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot58.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_21-0)

Petyr owns a slashed velvet doublet in cream-and-[silver](wiki:Silver),(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot33.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-0) a plum-colored doublet with a [mockingbird](wiki:Mockingbird) embroidered on the breast in black thread,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-0) a blue velvet tunic with puffed Petyr's chambers in the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) are located across the Great Yard from the [Tower of the Hand](wiki:Tower_of_the_Hand).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_29-0) He enjoys pomegranates(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos68.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-4) and drinks [wine](wiki:Wine).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_16-1)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Raffc41.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_30-0)

## Narrative Arc

### A Game of Thrones

Lady [Catelyn Stark](wiki:Catelyn_Stark) travels to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing) to inform her husband, Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark), about the attempt on the life of their son [Bran](wiki:Bran_Stark). Petyr, having learned of her arrival despite her efforts to remain unnoticed, intercepts Catelyn and has her brought to the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep). Catelyn is wary of Petyr and Lord [Varys](wiki:Varys) when she shows them the [Valyrian steel](wiki:Valyrian_steel) dagger used in the attempt by a [catspaw](wiki:Catspaw). Petyr says that he recognizes the dagger, claiming that it was once his but that he lost it to [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister) in a wager during the [tourney on Prince Joffrey's name day](wiki:Tourney_on_Prince_Joffrey's_name_day).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-13) In truth, however, [Littlefinger's blade](wiki:Littlefinger's_blade) had been lost to King [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Racok55.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_35-4)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rawoiafpetyr_baelish.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_14-9) Petyr promises Catelyn he will help her husband investigate the death of Lord [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn), Lord Stark's predecessor as [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_46-1)

After a meeting of the [small council](wiki:Small_council), Petyr brings Eddard to meet with his wife, whom Littlefinger has hidden at one of his [brothels](wiki:Prostitution), and Petyr convinces the new Hand, against his inclination, to make no move against the [Lannisters](wiki:House_Lannister) until they have proof. Catelyn feels she has reconnected with her childhood friend.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_46-2) Eddard remains wary of Petyr after Catelyn departs the capital, but he becomes more trusting after Littlefinger advises him to investigate Lord Arryn's household. Petyr, however, warns Ned not to trust anyone, including himself.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot25.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_49-0)

During the [Hand's tourney](wiki:Hand's_tourney), Petyr loses a hundred [golden dragons](wiki:Currency) to Lord [Renly Baratheon](wiki:Renly_Baratheon) when [Sandor Clegane](wiki:Sandor_Clegane) unhorses Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot30.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_29-1) Petyr meets [Sansa Stark](wiki:Sansa_Stark), the eldest daughter of Eddard and Catelyn, during the festivities, and he brushes the girl's cheek while touching her hair.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-5)

Acting on Petyr's lie about the dagger, Catelyn arrests Tyrion at the [inn at the crossroads](wiki:Inn_at_the_crossroads)(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot28.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_32-1) and takes him to the [Eyrie](wiki:Eyrie).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot34.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_33-1) Tyrion insists the dagger used by the assassin was not his, and he tells Catelyn that Petyr has often claimed at court to have taken her virginity.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot31.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_42-2)

When King Robert learns that the exiled Princess [Daenerys Targaryen](wiki:Daenerys_Targaryen) is pregnant, Petyr agrees with Robert in advocating her assassination. Ned resigns as Hand in protest of Robert's command, but before he can arrange to travel back to Winterfell, Petyr approaches him with the promise of assistance in his investigation into Jon Arryn's death.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot33.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_22-2) Littlefinger leads Ned to [a brothel](wiki:Chataya's_brothel) where [a young whore](wiki:Barra's_mother) is raising [one of Robert's bastard children](wiki:Barra). When Ned is accosted by [red cloaks](wiki:House_Lannister_guards) led by Ser [Jaime Lannister](wiki:Jaime_Lannister) while returning from the city shortly thereafter,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot35.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_18-2) Petyr flees to find [gold cloaks](wiki:City_Watch_of_King's_Landing), but Lord Stark is wounded and the [Hand's guards](wiki:House_Stark_guards) are killed in the meantime.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot39.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_50-0)

Petyr attends court while Eddard sits the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) in the Robert's absence, as the king has gone hunting in the [kingswood](wiki:Kingswood).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot43.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_51-0) Petyr's flight from Jaime's men bothers Lord Stark.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_23-2)

Robert is mortally wounded hunting a boar, and the dying king names Eddard as [Lord Regent](wiki:Regent) and [Protector of the Realm](wiki:Protector_of_the_Realm). Petyr advises Ned that despite their knowledge that Queen [Cersei Lannister](wiki:Cersei_Lannister)'s children are illegitimate and that Robert's brother, Lord [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), is the legal heir, the wisest course of action is to ensure Prince [Joffrey Baratheon](wiki:Joffrey_Baratheon)'s succession, a maneuver calculated to avoid all-out war and maximize their own power. Eddard refuses the suggestion and instead enlists Petyr's help in securing the allegiance of the gold cloaks for when he moves against Cersei.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot47.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_24-2) Littlefinger actually bribes the gold cloaks to support the queen in the [throne room](wiki:Throne_room), however. As he recovers his dagger during the [arrest of Eddard Stark](wiki:Arrest_of_Eddard_Stark), Petyr reminds Ned he had told him not to trust him.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_28-1)

Petyr wears black as the small council mourns Robert.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Rasos51.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_52-0) As the majority of Eddard's followers in King's Landing have been killed following his arrest,(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot50.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_53-0) Petyr takes [Vayon Poole](wiki:Vayon_Poole)'s daughter [Jeyne](wiki:Jeyne_Poole), Sansa's friend, into his custody.(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot51.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_54-0) Littlefinger mocks Ser [Barristan Selmy](wiki:Barristan_Selmy) when the famed [knight](wiki:Knight) is publicly dismissed from the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard).(wiki:Petyr_Baelish.cite_ref-Ragot57.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_55-0)

In that office, he taught the sons of Prince [Daeron Targaryen](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen) ([Baelor](wiki:Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II)), [Aerys](wiki:Aerys_I_Targaryen), [Rhaegel](wiki:Rhaegel_Targaryen) and [Maekar](wiki:Maekar_I_Targaryen)) as well as the [Great Bastards](wiki:Great_Bastards) ([Daemon Waters](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre), [Aegor Rivers](wiki:Aegor_Rivers) and [Brynden Rivers](wiki:Brynden_Rivers)) how to fight.(wiki:Quentyn_Ball.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-5) At some point, Aegon promised Quentyn a place in the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard) once an opening were to become apparent. Aegon died before an opening in the Sworn Brotherhood became vacant, however.(wiki:Quentyn_Ball.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii_targaryen.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)

Fireball was refused the white cloak for reasons unknown, and it was instead given to Ser [Willem Wylde](wiki:Willem_Wylde). This betrayal caused Quentyn to choose [Daemon Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre)'s side during Daemon's rebellion. It was rumored that Fireball had as much a part as [Bittersteel](wiki:Aegor_Rivers) in convincing Daemon to claim the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne).(wiki:Quentyn_Ball.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii_targaryen.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1)

Quentyn was the [master-at-arms](wiki:Master-at-arms) of the [Red Keep](wiki:Red_Keep) during King [Aegon IV Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_IV_Targaryen)'s days. In that office, he taught the sons of Prince [Daeron Targaryen](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen) ([Baelor](wiki:Baelor_Targaryen_(son_of_Daeron_II)), [Aerys](wiki:Aerys_I_Targaryen), [Rhaegel](wiki:Rhaegel_Targaryen) and [Maekar](wiki:Maekar_I_Targaryen)) as well as the [Great Bastards](wiki:Great_Bastards) ([Daemon Waters](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre), [Aegor Rivers](wiki:Aegor_Rivers) and [Brynden Rivers](wiki:Brynden_Rivers)) how to fight.(wiki:Quentyn_Ball.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-5) At some point, Aegon promised Quentyn a place in the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard) once an opening were to become apparent. In order to facilitate this Quentyn made his wife join the [silent sisters](wiki:Silent_sisters). Aegon died before an opening in the Sworn Brotherhood became vacant, however.(wiki:Quentyn_Ball.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii_targaryen.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)

### First Blackfyre Rebellion

When a place in the [Kingsguard](wiki:Kingsguard) became available under King [Daeron II Targaryen](wiki:Daeron_II_Targaryen), son and heir of Aegon IV, Quentyn reminded him of his father's promise. Fireball was refused the white cloak for reasons unknown, and it was instead given to Ser [Willem Wylde](wiki:Willem_Wylde). This betrayal caused Quentyn to choose [Daemon Blackfyre](wiki:Daemon_I_Blackfyre)'s side during Daemon's rebellion. It was rumored that Fireball had as much a part as [Bittersteel](wiki:Aegor_Rivers) in convincing Daemon to claim the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne).(wiki:Quentyn_Ball.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_targaryen_kings:_daeron_ii_targaryen.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1)

It was Fireball who rescued Daemon when King Daeron sent the Kingsguard to arrest him. During the [First Blackfyre Rebellion](wiki:First_Blackfyre_Rebellion), Quentyn killed Lord [Lefford](wiki:House_Lefford) at [Lannisport](wiki:Lannisport) and sent Lord [Damon Lannister](wiki:Damon_Lannister_(lord)) back to [Casterly Rock](wiki:Casterly_Rock) when he came out to confront him. At the crossing of the [Mander](wiki:Mander) he slew all the sons of Lady [Penrose](wiki:House_Penrose) save for the youngest, whom he spared as a kindness to her. Quentyn was slain by a common archer on the eve of the [Battle of the Redgrass Field](wiki:Battle_of_the_Redgrass_Field) when he stopped for a drink of water.(wiki:Quentyn_Ball.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)(wiki:Quentyn_Ball.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-6)

A young boy named [Glendon Flowers](wiki:Glendon_Flowers) believed himself to be Quentyn's [bastard](wiki:Bastard) son.(wiki:Quentyn_Ball.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-7)

## Appearances & Description

Quentyn was red-haired, with a fiery temper, hence the moniker *Fireball*.(wiki:Quentyn_Ball.cite_ref-Rtmk.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-4) He was considered to be one of the greatest [knights](wiki:Knight) of his day.(wiki:Quentyn_Ball.cite_ref-Rtss.7B.7B.7B2.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)
